This agrees with the sex of the native. But if the ayanamsa
value given by Lahiri is not accepted, the result can be differ

The time can be within a range ofr oughly twelve minutes.

3
Day-Constellation Complex
Of all the traditional methods one seems to be hitting the
hull's eye. Leaving aside the determination of the sex as per
the birth ghatis, we can consider the method given in Uttara
Kalamrita. This enables us to check the vighatis at the time of
birth. This can at times force us to correct the time by about
four minutes, as far as the constellation is concerned. The
time arrived at has to be checked to coincide with the weekday
of birth.
The Vighatis from Sun rise to the moment of birth have to
be multiplied by four and divided by nine. The remainder is to
be counted from Asvini, Magha, or Mula; or from Dhanishtha,
Mrigasira or Chitra. Mr. Rajagopala Iyyer wanted ns to add
one to the remainder.
